description:
Health Insurance porting is one very good right you have as a policy holder. However, it is very important to be extremely careful while porting your policy from one insurance company to another. Slight oversight might create big problems at the time of claims in future. 

This video will help you understand porting...
fulltitle: Health Insurance Porting | Don't Port Your Health Insurance | Understand the Risk in Porting

comments.text: Sir if I migrate my tata aig health insurance policy to another plan in the same company would I retain my moratorium period for PED and get back by Cumulative bonous? In 2022 I purchased my Tata Aig health insurance plan. Wanna to migrate now better plan in tata aig. Kindly suggest Sir.
comments.author: @thedancinggirlmeenakshimah7408

comments.text: If you migrate then you get the continuity of your all benefits related to waiting periods including PED.  moratorium & bonus
comments.author: @amarjitssaini

comments.text: I am 55, Wife -50 and 1 kid is 15 having a 10 years existing policy with 100 % Bonus NCB. No hospital  admission and Surgery.In last three years I have started sugar and thyroid medication. Now my weight is 89/5.7"height should I port to modern policy like no room caping and day care, and no deduction. Please advise
comments.author: @AMITKRCHAKRABORTY-v9c

comments.text: Diabetes & thyroid is acceptable condition with slight higher premium, so nothing to worry about it. I don’t know which policy you have thus can’t suggest, outrightly. However, best option is to migrate your policy to better plan in same company. If that’s not possible then port is ok but with word of caution that declare everything even if its smallest medical history. If you wish to discuss in details feel free to contact us at 8368742944
comments.author: @amarjitssaini

comments.text: buy one more dont fall for porting trap jo le liya vo shi h ya jis company mhe liya u usi ke plan mhe port krva lue khud ko
comments.author: @unbiased7197

comments.text: Can an individual paying premiums for a group policy of a bank port his policy because 18% gst on group policy premiums still continues & premiums for group policies are not at all cheaper
comments.author: @TapasBanerjee-y7x

comments.text: Yes, you can port your Group Health Policy to Individual health policy and take GST benefit.
comments.author: @amarjitssaini

comments.text: if I have taken policy from agent and agency is not competent to tackle claim, in that case the insured can do direct correspondce from insurer company yes or no
comments.author: @jayeshsinha7045

comments.text: Legal relationships is between you and the company, agent or broker is an intermediary between you and the company so connect with the company directly. If need any specific assistance please feel free to contact on 8368742944
comments.author: @amarjitssaini

comments.text: if the insurer does not allow migration from one plan to other can I complaint to ombudsman or what are option available since star health fho premium is higher of 2 adults due to some. chronic disease such as cronhs other company hesitate to accept porting proposal
comments.author: @jayeshsinha7045

comments.text: Ird already have guidlines that exusting benefits will be transferred to new insurer company
comments.author: @govla

comments.text: My family have no ped i want to buy new policy instead of porting, best porting is very expensive compare to buying new one.
comments.author: @ilbharat131313

comments.text: Please share details on 8368742944. Lets see what’s best can be done
comments.author: @amarjitssaini

comments.text:
Sir meri Helth policy ko grace period me port karna sahi Rahe ga kya ? (Aur GST ka benefit milega kya 22 September ke baad me )
Star se care company me port karna sahi hai ya nahi ?
comments.author: @ommandan8277

comments.text:
Porting after the expiry of the policy is not a good idea. As per regulations porting has to be done before expiry of the policy but if company is allowing after the expiry, it purely company's own decision. GST benefit is available to all the policies where coverage start date is 22nd Sep or later. 

About Star Health & Care Health both is good, I can't comment or give my recommendations without understanding full details. You your due diligence before buying or porting  the policy.
comments.author: @amarjitssaini

comments.text: Tata aig ki policy hai mere pas last year primium 37000 thi 49 age abhi 47000 ho gai jabki age 50hai next year 51 Mai slab change Hoga migration bhi nahi kar rahi company kya karna chahiye
comments.author: @n10jaiswal

comments.text:
Have you taken any claim in last couple of years or have developed some health conditions? If not, company will allow you to migrate to other products except the recently launched policy otherwise you can port out to some other company. 
If need personalised guidance, please call or message on 8368742944
comments.author: @amarjitssaini

comments.text: @amarjitssaini thanks sir

comments.text: You can increase the sum insured but do a proper due diligence before porting.

comments.text: Depends on your requirements, age, health etc. however both are equally good

comments.text: Let me know more about details of your policy on 8368742944

comments.text: Same question

comments.text: For last 4 years star health family health optima also increased my  premium

comments.text: @ Amarjitsaini
comments.author: @ashokkumaryadav5819

comments.text: Sir, i have already a health policy if want to purchase 2nd from other company, should I inform my 1st company regarding 2nd policy purchase. Thanks
comments.author: @SunilSharma-wk9uk

comments.text:
Hi, 
Not necessary in case of health insurance as it’s your choice to take claim from either of the policies. However, why waste money in 2 policies, increase your sum insured in existing policy itself. Assuming you buy a 10 lakhs policy for 18000 premium each then you pay 36000 for 2 policies where as for similar premium you might get a coverage of 40-50 lakhs if go for single policy. 

Need more information, please WhatsApp or call me on 8368742944
comments.author: @amarjitssaini

comments.text: Sure sir, Thank
comments.author: @SunilSharma-wk9uk

comments.text: मेरा हेल्थ इंश्योरेंस है icici lombard मैं उसका प्रीमियम बहुत महंगा होगया है इसके कारण मैं पोर्ट करवाना चाहता हो और मुझे knee रिप्लेसमेंट हुआ है हर्निया का ऑपरेशन हुआ और स्टोन का भी कल मुझे क्लेम मिलेगा या नहीं कि दिक्कत करेगा ?
comments.author: @nileshagarwal3994

comments.text:
अगर प्रीमियम के करण पालिसी पोर्ट करना चाहते हो तो मेरी राय में आपको पालिसी पोर्ट नहीं करना चाहिए क्योंकि जो पालिसी आज सस्ती होगी वह कल भी सस्ती रहेगी इसकी कोई गारंटी नहीं है।
जब भी आप पोर्ट करेंगे, कंपनी आप से सारी हेल्थ हिस्ट्री लेगी और उसके आधार पर डिसाइड करेगी की पोर्ट करनी है नहीं और किन शर्तों पर पोर्ट करनी है। उसी पर डिपेंड करे गा के आपको कौनसा क्लेम मिल सकता है।
comments.author: @amarjitssaini

comments.text: Sir my mother age is 69 and we have National insurance heath policy for more then 10 years having SA of Rs 5 lakh. But I am not satisfied with the company as they rejected the claim by giving non sense reasons many times. So I decided to port the policy as mother as Diabetes, Blood pressure and knee problem in both legs. So HDFC ergo and care rejected the port. Please suggest what to do as date of renewal is 30th Nov?
comments.author: @rahulnolakha4784

comments.text: It’s difficult to port sometimes with health history and renewal is due around the corner. I would recommend to renew the policy with National Insurance as of now. Next year before the renewal do a detailed research and port the policy.
comments.author: @amarjitssaini

comments.text: @amarjitssaini  thank you for suggestion.
comments.author: @rahulnolakha4784

comments.text:
Hello Sir, I have one question, 
Let say I have health insurance policy from company A since 5 years and I want to port to company B because of better features, but I also want continue with policy of company A even after porting to new company in order to have two policies of two different companies for extra safety
comments.author: @gauravshukla4329

comments.text: Continuity benefits will be with one policy only. If you decide to port then continuity will be with the ported policy and with your existing company you will have to take a new policy with fresh waiting periods.
comments.author: @amarjitssaini

comments.text: Nice and helpful
comments.author: @bijendradey9675

comments.text: Thank You!
comments.author: @amarjitssaini

comments.text: Sir hdfc ergo optima secure is worth for long term as per the high-rise of premium in future?..kindly guide
comments.author: @abhisheksinghal2586

comments.text: HDFC Ergo optima secure is good policy with up to 4 times of sum insured benefits. How much is sum insured is important to understand the suitability for future retirement
comments.author: @amarjitssaini

comments.text: ​ @amarjitssaini but sir what abt the high premium charged by company as compared to other insurers?
comments.author: @abhisheksinghal2586

comments.text: Sir, premium is not constant. In my 22 years of experience in General Insurance, I have seen many time that what is costly today becomes cheaper as other companies increase premium and same is the case in opposite as well when what is cheaper becomes costlier when company increase premium.
comments.author: @amarjitssaini

comments.text: Sir I am buy niva Bupa aspire titanium plan can this is a good health insurance for claim raise
comments.author: @souravdey8458

comments.text:
I ported my policy from one insurer to another after 6 years. My earlier sum assured was 7 Lacs & New company cover is 10 Lacs in that case i can use complete 10 lacs from 1st year itself or there are some other conditions applicable. 

Please guide
comments.author: @pankajpanchwagh4062

comments.text: Your sum insured plus cumulative bonus(if any) is the limit of liability of insurance. You will get claim up to 10 lacs. However, waiting period continuity will be limited to 7 lacs, that means any claim related to specified diseases/treatments and pre existing diseases related claims up to 7 lacs and other claim up to 10 lacs is payable
comments.author: @amarjitssaini

comments.text: @amarjitssaini  Is 7 Lacs cover applicable from day 1 ?
comments.author: @pankajpanchwagh4062

comments.text:
Theoretically - Yes
Practically, can comment only after having a look at the policy. If you need further assistance please share your policy on WhatsApp no 8368742944. We will call and discuss finer details of your policy coverage
comments.author: @amarjitssaini

comments.text: thanx for clearing doubt with example.
comments.author: @kids_entertainment007

comments.text: Thank You!
comments.author: @amarjitssaini

comments.text:
Hi sir 
01 Question hai 
5 year ka moretorium period complete karne ke baad company claim reject nahi karti to 5 year bad acchi featured or sasti policy me port Kiya to chalega kya ?
Means ab hdfc or bad me niva Bupa nahi to star health assure
comments.author: @ganeshl97

comments.text:
Sir, 
At the time of porting company will re underwrite your proposal to port and you will have to declare everything related to your health in this case if, god forbid, you have developed some medical condition company might not port your policy. Secondly, if you don’t declare and port your policy and company can prove that it’s an old condition which you suppressed you will not get claims and also your policy will be terminated. So it is better to buy insurance with future requirements in mind. Remember insurance is for uncertainties and if there is a certainty of claims, there is no insurance.
comments.author: @amarjitssaini

comments.text: You are absolutely wrong because as per the new guidelines by IRDAI, in case of porting after 5 years, whether you declare any disease or not, the claim cannot be rejected except in case of fraud.
comments.author: @ArunSingh-bu1dx

comments.text: I am happy to be wrong here but if you don’t declare and company can prove that this concealment was deliberate, it falls under fraud so as an expert my suggest don’t take things for granted and follow the process. Otherwise, you are free to do whatever you want, consequences will be yours.
comments.author: @amarjitssaini
